_: Canonical Daylight DC-1 facts. Every tool in the skill pack reads this file; correct it here and the whole pack recalibrates. Fields marked measured:false are educated defaults awaiting a reading from a real device — see site/calibrate.html.

device.name: Daylight DC-1
device.os_note: a newer Android base is expected — on OS bumps re-check: predictive back (onBackPressed migration), targetSdk in the shell manifest, WebView behavior changes

refresh.note: variable-refresh IGZO; pixel response time vs refresh rate unresolved — motion blur reported at high rates; investigation prompt: REFRESH-MOTION-PROMPT.md

panel.type: LivePaper: transflective TN LCD, IGZO oxide TFT backplane, no color filter array (e-paper-class, NOT e-ink)
panel.backlight: amber (blue-free), transmissive through the transflective panel — a backlight, not a front-light
panel.tn_note: TN mode: gamma shifts with vertical viewing angle (worst case grayscale inversion at steep tilt) — verify designs at ±30°; another reason adjacent mid-grays must not carry meaning

gray_levels_practical.note: eyes resolve ~9-16 in ambient light; count on calibrate.html

devicePixelRatio.note: read from calibrate.html on a real DC-1

curve._: LivePaper simulation: luminance -> shadow-crushing gamma -> compressed range -> paper tint. Tuned by eye, not measured.

night.note: review photos suggest the real amber is considerably MORE saturated than this tint, and backlight warmth is user-adjustable — recalibrate from photos of calibrate.html at min/max warmth

ramp._: the default five-tone UI ramp (club-site flavor; see solos_scale for the design system's)

solos_scale._: the Daylight/Sol:OS design system's Day-mode grays — authoritative for Sol:OS-native dishes (see daylight-design/references/design-system.md)

hk.note: used by daylight-map; L_a default from Smith et al. 2008 — the right value for a reflective panel in ambient light, and the object-vs-luminous variant choice, await hardware validation
